page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and 8-bit integer operations. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The local storage capacity makes it suitable for workloads requiring high-speed temporary disk space, though persistent storage must be attached separately if required beyond the local disk allocation.

In benchmark testing, the D64ds_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a PassMark CPU score of 86642.75 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 67260.4. Single-threaded performance is also notable, with gzip compression reaching 36.06 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 15.17 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust, measuring 326.02 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 127.56 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves 111.2 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 104.9 GB/sec.

Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and substantial local storage, the D64ds_v7 is positioned for database operations, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, as evidenced by its prompt processing speed of 3946.15 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model. Qualitatively, the hardware profile offers high resource density for multi-threaded applications, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. The instance is equipped with 3780 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, though it lacks a dedicated GPU. In performance testing, the D64ds_v7 delivers strong multi-core results, including a PassMark CPU score of 86642.75 and a static web serving throughput of 104.9 GB/sec. It also demonstrates high efficiency in database operations, processing 111.2 million Redis SET operations per second. This instance is suited for relational databases, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage on an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU x86_64 platform featuring 256 GB RAM, substantial local storage, and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed.
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Delivers strong multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 86642.75.
Achieves high web serving throughput of 104.9 GB/sec and 111.2 million Redis SET operations per second.
Optimized for relational databases, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
